teachingPoint: IR-T605-1 — T605 TenantProvisioningOrchestrator MUST use SETNX at ORDER 1 to guarantee idempotent bootstrap. Without SETNX, concurrent provisioning requests for the same tenantId can produce duplicate tenant records and double-seed FREEDOM config. bulkSeed MUST be synchronous — async scatter with partial completion leaves the tenant in a half-initialized state that downstream services cannot safely operate on. On any failure, TenantProvisioningFailed MUST be emitted before returning so consumers can trigger compensating actions.
positiveExample: T605: redis.SETNX('provision_lock:{tenantId}', 'locked') at ORDER 1 — returns early with TenantProvisioningFailed if lock already held → bulkSeed(freedomDefaults, {sync: true}) → storeDocument(tenantRecord) → emit TenantProvisioned. TenantProvisioningFailed emitted on any error at any step.
negativeExample: T605 calls storeDocument first, then attempts SETNX after — two concurrent requests both pass storeDocument and create duplicate tenant records. Or T605 calls Promise.all([seedA, seedB, seedC]) async scatter — a seedB failure leaves tenantId with partial FREEDOM config that causes runtime errors in config lookups.
discriminatingConstraint: SETNX at ORDER 1 before any write, bulkSeed synchronous — any reorder or async scatter = BUILD_FAILURE.
keywords: SETNX ORDER 1 tenant provisioning idempotent bulkSeed synchronous TenantProvisioningFailed atomic bootstrap T605 TenantProvisioningOrchestrator concurrent lock

teachingPoint: IR-T606-1 — T606 TenantConfigurationManager MUST define MACHINE_LOCKED_KEYS as a compile-time constant set. Any attempt to write a key that appears in MACHINE_LOCKED_KEYS MUST be rejected at ORDER 1 before any storage operation. Locked keys protect engine-critical config values that, if overwritten by tenants, would break fabric routing and security gates. The rejection path MUST NOT attempt to store the document — TenantConfigKeyImmutable MUST be emitted on rejection. There is no FREEDOM override for locked key enforcement.
positiveExample: T606: const MACHINE_LOCKED_KEYS = new Set(['system.fabric.router','security.occ.enabled']) as const at module level → validateKeyNotLocked(key) at ORDER 1 → if locked return DataProcessResult.failure + emit ConfigKeyImmutable → storeDocument(configRecord, {occ: true}) → emit TenantConfigurationUpdated.
negativeExample: T606 reads locked key list from FREEDOM config at runtime — an operator can accidentally remove a key from the locked set, allowing tenant writes to engine-critical keys. Or T606 allows the write but emits a warning — the write must be blocked entirely, not warned.
discriminatingConstraint: MACHINE_LOCKED_KEYS compile-time constant, ORDER 1 rejection with no storage attempt — runtime list or warn-not-block = BUILD_FAILURE.
keywords: MACHINE_LOCKED_KEYS compile-time constant ORDER 1 rejection ConfigKeyImmutable OCC write-only T606 TenantConfigurationManager engine-critical keys locked

teachingPoint: IR-T607-1 — T607 TenantQuotaMaterializer MUST use Redis MULTI/EXEC to write all quota keys atomically. A loop of individual SET calls is prohibited because a process crash between iterations leaves partial quota state where some limits are applied and others are not. Quota values MUST derive from tier definitions in FREEDOM config — never hardcoded constants. The quota key filter MUST exclude non-quota keys to prevent over-writing unrelated Redis entries during materialization.
positiveExample: T607: reads tierDefinition from freedom.getConfig('tenant_quota_tier_{tier}') → builds quotaKeyMap from tier definition → redis.MULTI() → for each [key, value] quotaKeyMap.entries(): multi.SET(key, value) → redis.EXEC() → emit TenantQuotaMaterialized with keyCount.
negativeExample: T607 uses for await (const [k, v] of quotaMap) { await redis.set(k, v) } — individual SET loop; crash after 3 of 7 keys leaves tenant with partial quotas enforced. Or T607 uses hardcoded quota values like { api_calls: 10000, storage_gb: 50 } — quota changes require code deployment rather than FREEDOM config update.
discriminatingConstraint: Redis MULTI/EXEC for all quota keys in one transaction, values from tier FREEDOM config — individual SET loop or hardcoded values = BUILD_FAILURE.
keywords: Redis MULTI EXEC quota atomic T607 TenantQuotaMaterializer no individual SET loop tier definitions FREEDOM config quota key filter TenantQuotaMaterialized

teachingPoint: IR-T608-1 — T608 TenantLifecycleManager MUST use updateDocument (suspend-not-delete) for tenant suspension. Deleting a tenant record during suspension destroys the audit trail and makes recovery impossible. Suspension MUST cascade to active subscriptions via cascadeToSubscriptions: true. Data purge is a separate privileged operation handled via DataPurgeRequested event delegation — T608 MUST NOT perform direct data deletion. TenantSuspended event MUST carry cascadeToSubscriptions: true to notify downstream processors.
positiveExample: T608: updateDocument(tenantId, { status: 'SUSPENDED', suspendedAt: now }, {occ: true}) → emit TenantSuspended with { tenantId, cascadeToSubscriptions: true } → downstream subscription handler processes cascadeToSubscriptions. DataPurgeRequested emitted only during termination flow, never during suspension.
negativeExample: T608 calls deleteDocument(tenantId) during suspension — destroys audit trail and makes unsuspend impossible. Or T608 enqueues DataPurgeRequested on suspension — conflates reversible suspension with irreversible purge, risks destroying data that should be retained for recovery.
discriminatingConstraint: updateDocument ONLY for suspension, cascadeToSubscriptions:true in TenantSuspended, DataPurgeRequested only on termination — deleteDocument or purge on suspend = BUILD_FAILURE.
keywords: suspend not delete updateDocument cascadeToSubscriptions T608 TenantLifecycleManager TenantSuspended DataPurgeRequested delegation termination audit trail recovery

teachingPoint: IR-T605-3 — All FLOW-15 services MUST resolve tenantId exclusively from AsyncLocalStorage (ALS) or an internal hash derivation. tenantId MUST NEVER appear as a request payload parameter. Accepting tenantId from the request payload enables tenant impersonation — a caller can submit any tenantId and operate as that tenant. ALS is populated by the fabric middleware from the authenticated session token before any service method is invoked.
positiveExample: T605/T606/T607/T608: const tenantId = this.tenantContext.getTenantId() — resolved from ALS. No tenantId parameter accepted in the service method signature or validated from request body. Fabric middleware sets ALS before dispatch.
negativeExample: T605 accepts tenantId in the event payload as provisionRequest.tenantId and uses it directly — a compromised producer can inject any tenantId and provision or reconfigure arbitrary tenants. Or T606 validates tenantId from request body against ALS 'for safety' — the validation itself signals that tenantId can come from the payload.
discriminatingConstraint: tenantId from ALS only — any service accepting tenantId as payload parameter = BUILD_FAILURE.
keywords: tenantId ALS AsyncLocalStorage never payload parameter tenant impersonation scope isolation T605 T606 T607 T608 fabric middleware session token

teachingPoint: IR-T608-3 — T608 TenantLifecycleManager MUST delegate all data purge operations to DataPurgeRequested event emission during termination — never perform direct deletion. The tombstoneRef MUST be a one-way hash of the tenantId — never the raw tenantId — to prevent reverse lookup of purged tenant identities. This creates an immutable evidence record that purge was requested without retaining the original identifier. The termination flow is irreversible; any accidental direct deletion during termination bypasses the compliance audit chain.
positiveExample: T608 termination path: updateDocument(tenantId, { status: 'TERMINATED', terminatedAt: now }) → tombstoneRef = crypto.createHash('sha256').update(tenantId).digest('hex') → emit DataPurgeRequested { tombstoneRef } → emit TenantTerminated { tombstoneRef } — raw tenantId never in either event payload.
negativeExample: T608 calls deleteDocument(tenantId) directly during termination — bypasses DataPurgeRequested compliance chain. Or T608 includes raw tenantId in DataPurgeRequested event — allows downstream processor to reverse-identify the purged tenant from event logs.
discriminatingConstraint: DataPurgeRequested event delegation with tombstoneRef one-way hash — direct deletion or raw tenantId in purge event = BUILD_FAILURE.
keywords: DataPurgeRequested termination tombstoneRef one-way hash sha256 T608 TenantLifecycleManager TenantTerminated compliance purge delegation no direct deletion
